---
title: "ED Price Transparency Tool"
subtitle: "Hillsborough County, FL"
author: "GLHLTH 562 · Duke University"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    highlight-style: github
    code-line-numbers: false
    footer: "GLHLTH 562 · ED Price Transparency · Hillsborough County, FL"
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    smaller: true
    scrollable: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# What problem does this product address? {background-color="#0F6E56"}

---

## The problem {background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Emergency care is the most opaque healthcare purchase in America

A chest pain workup in Hillsborough County can cost

::: {.fragment}
**$3,060** at South Florida Baptist
:::
::: {.fragment}
**$4,390** at AdventHealth
:::
::: {.fragment}
for **identical services** — a difference of **$1,330**
:::
::: {.fragment}
<br>
Patients have no way to know this **before they go.**
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
| Hospital | Cash price |
|----------|-----------|
| Tampa General | $4,118 |
| St. Joseph's | $3,640 |
| AdventHealth | **$4,390** |
| HCA Brandon | $3,520 |
| HCA S. Tampa | $3,710 |
| HCA W. Tampa | $3,540 |
| S. FL Baptist | **$3,060** |

: Cash prices — chest pain workup {.striped}
:::
:::
:::

---

## Why does this gap exist? {background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**The data is public — but unusable**

Since 2021, CMS requires all hospitals to publish machine-readable files (MRFs) of every charge under **45 CFR Part 180**

But for a typical patient these files are:

::: {.fragment}
- Up to **2 GB** per hospital
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Full of cryptic codes: `CPT 84484`
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Impossible to compare across hospitals
:::
::: {.fragment}
- No plain-language explanations
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
### Who this tool is for

- **Uninsured patients** choosing between EDs on cost
- **Underinsured patients** with high deductibles
- **Anyone** who wants to understand their bill before or after a visit
- **Health policy researchers** studying price variation in Tampa Bay
:::
:::
:::

---

## Our solution {background-color="#065A82"}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Turn a symptom into a price comparison in under 5 seconds
:::

::: {.columns style="margin-top: 1rem;"}
::: {.column width="22%"}
::: {.fragment}
**1. Type a symptom**

"chest pain"
:::
:::
::: {.column width="5%"}
→
:::
::: {.column width="22%"}
::: {.fragment}
**2. Claude maps to billing codes**

CPT 99285, 93010, 84484
:::
:::
::: {.column width="5%"}
→
:::
::: {.column width="22%"}
::: {.fragment}
**3. Prices from 7 hospital MRFs**

Updated weekly automatically
:::
:::
::: {.column width="5%"}
→
:::
::: {.column width="19%"}
::: {.fragment}
**4. Ranked results**

With your insurance and ZIP
:::
:::
:::

---

# Live demo {background-color="#0F6E56"}

::: {style="text-align: center; color: white; margin-top: 2rem; font-size: 1.4rem;"}
**https://clukeduke.shinyapps.io/ed-price-transparency/**
:::

---

## What the app does — Search tab

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**User inputs:**

- Free-text symptom search
- Quick-select buttons for 7 common conditions
- ZIP code for live distance calculation
- Distance slider to filter far hospitals
- Insurance plan selector (8 Florida insurers)
- Sort by cash price, gross charge, or distance

**GenAI in action:**

When a symptom is not in the local dataset, the app calls the **Anthropic Claude API** which returns CPT/HCPCS/DRG codes as structured JSON
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**What you see:**

- Three value boxes: lowest price, highest price, potential savings
- Sortable hospital cards showing:
  - Your estimated price (cash or insurer rate)
  - Gross charge
  - Negotiated rate range
  - Distance from your ZIP
- Itemized service breakdown per hospital
- **Hover tooltips** explaining every billing code in plain language
:::
:::

---

## What the app does — Map, Compare, Calculator

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
### 🗺️ Map tab

Interactive **Leaflet map** of all 7 Hillsborough County EDs

- Click any pin for address and price
- Prices update when you change insurer or search a new condition
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
### 📊 Compare tab

Two chart types for price comparison:

- **Dot plot** — shows cash price AND gross charge per hospital
- **Bar chart** — traditional view

Two chart types for cost breakdown:

- **Treemap** — shows which services dominate the bill
- **Stacked bars** — traditional breakdown
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
### 🧮 Calculator tab

Out-of-pocket estimator:

- Annual deductible
- Amount already met
- Coinsurance percentage
- Out-of-pocket maximum

Calculates what you **actually pay** after insurance
:::
:::

---

## Treatment definitions

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**The problem within the problem:**

Even if a patient finds the price data, they still see `CPT 84484 — Troponin I` and have no idea what it means.

**The solution:**

Every billing code badge and service line item has a hover tooltip with:

1. **What it is** — full name
2. **What it does** — plain-language description
3. **Why you see this** — why it appears on an ED bill
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Example — Troponin I:**

> *Measures a protein released into the bloodstream when heart muscle cells are damaged. Elevated levels strongly indicate a heart attack. Results typically return in 1–2 hours.*

> **Why you see this:** The definitive blood test for ruling a heart attack in or out. Cannot be skipped for a serious chest pain workup — you may be billed for it twice if a repeat draw is ordered.

20 treatments defined across all 7 conditions
:::
:::

---

## Three capabilities — rubric requirements met

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
### 🔌 API integration

- CMS MRFs fetched programmatically via `httr2`
- Anthropic Claude API called at query time
- No manual downloads ever required
- Hospitals legally prohibited from blocking automated access under CMS rules
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
### 🤖 GenAI in pipeline

- Claude performs **real clinical classification**
- Symptom → structured JSON with CPT/HCPCS/DRG codes
- Degrades gracefully when API unavailable
- The app cannot function without it
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
### ⚙️ Automation

- GitHub Actions runs **every Monday 6 AM UTC**
- Re-downloads all 7 hospital MRFs
- Filters to ED-relevant codes
- Commits updated prices to repo
- Uploads processing log as artifact
:::
:::

---

# Key design decisions and trade-offs {background-color="#065A82"}

---

## Decision 1: Static data + live Claude API

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**What we chose:**

Pre-process MRF data into a static JSON file via a weekly pipeline. Call Claude API only for unknown symptoms at query time.

**Why:**

- Hospital MRF files are 100MB–2GB each
- Downloading 7 files on every search would be unusably slow
- Pre-processing keeps response time under 1 second
- Claude handles the long tail of uncommon symptoms
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**The trade-off:**

Prices are up to 7 days old rather than real-time.

For hospital pricing data that is updated annually, this is acceptable — hospital prices don't change week to week.

**What we gave up:**

Real-time MRF fetching would guarantee prices are always current. A production version with server-side caching could bridge this gap.
:::
:::

---

## Decision 2: Payer multipliers instead of raw MRF payer data

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**What we chose:**

Apply payer-specific multipliers to the cash price rather than parsing the full payer-by-plan rates from each MRF.

```r
get_payer_price <- function(base_cash, hosp_id, payer_id) {
  if (payer_id == "cash") return(base_cash)
  multiplier <- PAYER_MULTIPLIERS[[hosp_id]][[payer_id]]
  round(base_cash * multiplier)
}
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Why:**

Each MRF contains hundreds of inconsistent plan name variations per insurer across hospitals — normalizing these requires a separate NLP classification step.

**The trade-off:**

Multipliers give clinically reasonable estimates but are not exact. The disclaimer on every results page is essential.

**What we gave up:**

Exact negotiated rates per plan — the most valuable data in the MRF.
:::
:::

---

## Decision 3: Dot plot and treemap over bar charts

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Why dot plot:**

Bars imply the area matters — for prices, only the endpoint matters. The dot plot shows both cash price and gross charge with a connector line, making the gap between them the visual story.

**Why treemap:**

For abdominal pain, the CT scan is 60% of the total bill. A stacked bar obscures this. The treemap makes the CT scan rectangle dominate visually — the insight is immediate.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**The trade-off:**

Dot plots are less familiar to general audiences than bar charts. Some users may need a moment to understand the encoding.

**Solution:**

We kept both chart types toggleable via a radio button. Users who prefer bar charts can switch — the dot plot is the default because it tells a better story.
:::
:::

---

# What we would improve with more time {background-color="#1a1a2e"}

---

## Improvement 1: Real payer-specific rates

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**What we have now:**

Multipliers applied to cash price — reasonable estimates based on Florida market data

**What we would build:**

A proper MRF payer parsing pipeline that uses Claude to classify inconsistent plan names into standard insurer categories, then stores actual negotiated rates by insurer and hospital.

**Why it matters:**

The difference between "estimated UnitedHealthcare rate" and "actual UnitedHealthcare PPO negotiated rate at Tampa General" is the difference between a useful tool and a definitive one.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
**Example of the problem:**

Tampa General's MRF contains plan entries like:

```
United Healthcare Choice Plus
UHC Choice
UnitedHealthcare Options PPO
UHC Navigate HMO
United Choice Navigator
```

All referring to UnitedHealthcare but stored inconsistently. Claude could classify these reliably — we just ran out of time to build the pipeline.
:::
:::

---

## Improvement 2: Driving time + urgent care comparison

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Driving time

**What we have:** straight-line Haversine distance from ZIP centroid

**What we would build:** Google Maps Distance Matrix API returning **driving time in minutes** — far more relevant for emergency care

"8 miles" means nothing in an emergency. "14 minutes" is actionable.

**Why we didn't:** requires Google Maps API key with billing enabled — cost risk for a class project.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Urgent care comparison

Many ED conditions can be treated at urgent care for a fraction of the cost:

| Condition | ED cash | Urgent care |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| Laceration | $1,185–$1,660 | $150–$300 |
| Broken arm | $1,910–$2,640 | $200–$400 |
| Headache | $2,440–$3,360 | $100–$200 |

The most impactful price transparency intervention is "should you go to an ED at all?"
:::
:::

---

## Summary {background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**What we built:**

A working R Shiny app that turns a plain-language symptom into a ranked price comparison across 7 Hillsborough County EDs — with insurance filtering, distance filtering, out-of-pocket calculation, an interactive map, and plain-language treatment definitions

**Three capabilities:**

- ✅ API integration (CMS MRFs + Claude API)
- ✅ GenAI in pipeline (symptom → billing codes)
- ✅ Automation (GitHub Actions weekly refresh)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**The core insight:**

The data was always public. The problem was never access — it was usability.

::: {.fragment}
A patient typing "chest pain" should not need to know what `CPT 84484` means.

They should just see:

**South Florida Baptist: $3,060**

**AdventHealth: $4,390**

**You could save $1,330 by driving 8 more miles.**
:::
:::
:::

---

## {background-color="#0F6E56"}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Thank you
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; color: white; margin-top: 1.5rem;"}
**Live app:** https://clukeduke.shinyapps.io/ed-price-transparency/

**GitHub:** github.com/CLukeDuke/ed-price-transparency
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.75); margin-top: 1.5rem; font-size: 0.85rem;"}
Built with R · Shiny · httr2 · leaflet · plotly · reactable · Anthropic Claude API

CMS Hospital Price Transparency data (45 CFR Part 180) · Duke University GLHLTH 562
:::
